description: Chapter 3 - Construction of quantum theory II: matrix mechanics. A guided auxiliary structure for Stern-Gerlach physics, Dirac notation, Hilbert-space vectors, matrix operators, probabilities, commutators, time evolution and the bridge back to wave mechanics.

topics.title: Why matrix mechanics is needed
topics.note: Chapter map: why the vector-matrix language is natural for discrete quantum systems such as spin.

topics.title: Stern-Gerlach experiment: magnetic force and spin
topics.note: Inhomogeneous magnetic fields, magnetic moments, two spots and the interpretation of spin as a two-level observable.

topics.title: Dirac notation: kets, bras and inner products
topics.note: Postulate for quantum states in vector form: a physical state is represented by a ket in Hilbert space.

topics.title: Hilbert space, bases, superposition and completeness
topics.note: Orthonormal bases, vector expansion, spin bases along different axes and the meaning of coefficients as projections.

topics.title: Operators as matrices
topics.note: Postulate for observables and operators: measurable quantities are represented by Hermitian operators acting on state vectors.

topics.title: Eigenvalues, eigenstates and spectral decomposition
topics.note: How measurement outcomes enter the eigenvalue equation, and how an operator is rebuilt from its eigenbasis.

topics.title: Pauli matrices and spin observables
topics.note: Spin-1/2 operators, the z and x bases, and the Pauli matrices as the working example of matrix mechanics.

topics.title: Born rule in vector notation
topics.note: Postulate of probability: amplitudes are inner products, and probabilities are squared moduli of projections.

topics.title: Expectation value, variance and preparation
topics.note: Ensemble averages, uncertainty of an observable and how repeated Stern-Gerlach measurements prepare a state.

topics.title: Hermitian operators and real outcomes
topics.note: Why observables must have real eigenvalues, and how Hermitian conjugation controls the matrix representation.

topics.title: Commutators, compatibility and measurement order
topics.note: Non-commuting spin components, compatible observables and why an intermediate measurement can erase information.

topics.title: Schrodinger picture: unitary time evolution
topics.note: Time-dependent state vectors, the unitary evolution operator and phase evolution in the energy basis.

topics.title: Heisenberg picture: operator evolution
topics.note: Fixed state vectors, time-dependent operators and the Heisenberg equation of motion.

topics.title: Unitary transformations and change of basis
topics.note: How states and operators transform between bases while probabilities and expectation values remain invariant.

topics.title: Operator functions and chapter synthesis
topics.note: Matrix exponentials, functions of operators and the correspondence between wave mechanics and matrix mechanics.
